message:
chore(beads): close hs8w (br SQLite DB rebuilt from JSONL)

Recovery procedure that worked: stop using `--db <separate-temp-path>`
(which hung on the original codex-beads-repair attempt), move the
canonical `.beads/beads.db{,-wal}` aside, run `br init --prefix
frankenredis`, then `br sync --import-only --rebuild` — which imported
all 805 records cleanly. Post-rebuild `br doctor`: sqlite.integrity_check
OK, counts.db_vs_jsonl OK, sync.metadata in sync.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenredis-hs04.1","title":"Audit SCAN/KEYS/SORT ordering assumptions before BTreeMap→HashMap migration","description":"# Audit Ordering Assumptions

## What

Before replacing BTreeMap with HashMap in Store::entries, systematically identify
every code path that depends on BTreeMap's sorted iteration order. These must be
adapted for HashMap's non-deterministic iteration.

## Specific Areas to Audit

### 1. SCAN / HSCAN / SSCAN / ZSCAN
- Location: crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs — search for scan-related functions
- Current behavior: likely iterates BTreeMap in sorted order with a cursor
  that's a key-position offset
- Redis behavior: uses reversed-bit-increment cursor over hash bucket indices;
  no ordering guarantee; may return duplicates; guarantees full coverage of
  keys that exist throughout the entire scan
- Action needed: re-implement cursor as either epoch-based (last-seen-key) or
  snapshot-based

### 2. KEYS pattern matching
- Location: crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs — search for keys_matching or similar
- Current behavior: returns keys in BTreeMap sorted order
- Redis behavior: no ordering guarantee
- Action needed: verify no conformance tests depend on sorted KEYS output;
  if they do, mark as over-specified and fix tests

### 3. SORT command with BY nosort
- Location: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs — SORT handler
- Current behavior: NOSORT returns elements in "natural order"
- Redis behavior: for lists, NOSORT returns insertion order (list-internal);
  for sets, returns arbitrary order; doesn't depend on entries map order
- Action needed: verify SORT doesn't use entries iteration order

### 4. state_digest computation
- Location: crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:7875
- Current behavior: iterates entries in sorted order, chains FNV hashes
- Action needed: MUST change to order-independent algorithm (XOR-of-per-entry-
  hashes) regardless. This is also a prerequisite for frankenredis-n5xv.
- Note: state_digest is now on the cold path (lazy-digest refactor), so the
  algorithm change is safe; the only concern is digest stability for conformance
  fixtures that check evidence-ledger digests. Search for hard-coded digest
  values in golden_outputs/ and conformance fixtures.

### 5. DEBUG / OBJECT / internal dump paths
- Any path that iterates all keys and depends on order

### 6. Conformance fixtures
- Search golden_outputs/ for any fixture that asserts key ordering
- Check if any conformance test sorts keys differently or depends on
  deterministic iteration

## Deliverable

A markdown checklist of every ordering-dependent path, with the required
adaptation strategy for each. This checklist becomes the implementation
guide for the migration subtask.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Complete audit document covering all 6 areas above
2. No unidentified ordering dependency in the codebase
3. Each dependency has a named adaptation strategy","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:24:33.024141262Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T20:43:00.602794686Z","closed_at":"2026-04-09T20:43:00.602582915Z","close_reason":"Audit completed; ordering dependencies documented and implementation strategy chosen","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["perf","store"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-hs04.1","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-hs04","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:24:33.024141262Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"frankenredis-hs04.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"[CalmOak 2026-04-09] Audit summary: deterministic ordering dependencies are confirmed in runtime KEYS/SCAN, store active-expire cursoring, state_digest, RDB snapshot export via all_keys(), and unit/conformance tests that hard-code sorted KEYS/SCAN output. Implementation path: HashMap for Store::entries plus an auxiliary ordered key index to preserve deterministic cold-path behavior while moving hot-path lookups off BTreeMap.","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:38:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-hs04.2","title":"Implement HashMap migration for Store::entries","description":"# Implement HashMap Migration

## What

Replace BTreeMap<Vec<u8>, Entry> with HashMap<Vec<u8>, Entry> at
crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:772 and adapt all ordering-dependent code paths
identified in the audit subtask (frankenredis-hs04.1).

## Key Implementation Steps

### 1. Change the type declaration
```rust
// Before:
entries: BTreeMap<Vec<u8>, Entry>,

// After:
entries: HashMap<Vec<u8>, Entry>,
```

### 2. Fix SCAN cursor
Implement epoch-based cursor strategy:
- SCAN 0 starts a new iteration, returns a batch of N keys + next cursor
- Cursor encodes the iteration state (e.g., hash of last-seen key + count)
- On SCAN <cursor>, find position and return next batch
- Guarantee: all keys present throughout full scan are returned (may duplicate)

### 3. Fix state_digest
Change from sequential FNV chain to XOR-of-per-entry-hashes:
```rust
pub fn state_digest(&self) -> String {
    let mut hash = 0_u64;
    for (key, entry) in &self.entries {
        hash ^= entry_hash(key, entry);
    }
    format!("{hash:016x}")
}
```

### 4. Fix any test assertions
Update tests that depend on sorted key iteration order.

### 5. Consider HashMap capacity hints
Pre-size the HashMap based on expected keyspace. The default load factor
(7/8 for std HashMap) is fine; don't over-optimize.

## Files to Change

- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs — primary change (entries type + all methods)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs — if any iteration depends on order
- golden_outputs/ — if any fixtures have order-dependent expected output

## This Must Come After

The audit subtask (frankenredis-hs04.1) must complete first to identify all
adaptation points. Implementing without the audit risks silent ordering bugs
that only surface in production under specific key distributions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:24:48.507423683Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T21:06:44.135030120Z","closed_at":"2026-04-09T21:06:44.134737927Z","close_reason":"Implemented and validated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["perf","store"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-hs04.2","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-hs04","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:24:48.507423683Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-hs04.2","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-hs04.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:27:27.473696763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":5,"issue_id":"frankenredis-hs04.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"[CalmOak 2026-04-09] Implementation started. Store::entries now uses HashMap with a parallel ordered key index reserved for deterministic global-iteration paths (KEYS/SCAN helpers, active-expire cursoring, state_digest, snapshot/export helpers). Remote fr-store validation passed, and remote workspace check completed successfully before artifact retrieval tailed.","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:43:25Z"},{"id":6,"issue_id":"frankenredis-hs04.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"[CalmOak 2026-04-09] Implementation started. Store::entries now uses HashMap with a parallel ordered key index reserved for deterministic global-iteration paths (KEYS/SCAN helpers, active-expire cursoring, state_digest, snapshot/export helpers). Remote  passed, and remote workspace cargo check completed successfully before artifact retrieval tailed.","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:43:38Z"},{"id":7,"issue_id":"frankenredis-hs04.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"[CalmOak 2026-04-09] Implementation complete: Store::entries now uses HashMap with a BTreeSet ordered index for deterministic global iteration. Validation passed via rch-offloaded cargo check --workspace --all-targets, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test -p fr-conformance -- --nocapture; cargo fmt --check passed locally.","created_at":"2026-04-09T21:06:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-hs04.3","title":"Benchmark + conformance gate for HashMap migration","description":"# Benchmark + Conformance Gate for HashMap Migration

## What

After frankenredis-hs04.2 (HashMap implementation) is complete, run the full
quality gate and produce a before/after delta artifact.

## Steps

1. Build release binary with HashMap changes
2. Run fr-bench for all workloads: set, get, incr, mixed (p1 and p16)
3. Capture perf flamegraph to confirm BTreeMap::insert and memcmp overhead
   is reduced
4. Run cargo test --workspace (all unit tests)
5. Run cargo test -p fr-conformance (all 43 conformance tests)
6. Verify SCAN works correctly with a multi-cursor full-scan test
7. Compare throughput numbers to the April 9 baseline
8. Write isomorphism proof artifact at
   artifacts/optimization/hashmap-migration/ISOMORPHISM_PROOF.md

## Expected Outcome

BTreeMap::insert (2.93%) and __memcmp_avx2_movbe (12.73%) should both drop
significantly. Combined ~15% CPU savings → estimated 10-15% throughput
improvement. SET p1 should move from 75K to ~85K+ ops/sec.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 43 conformance tests pass
2. All workspace tests pass
3. Benchmark shows measurable improvement (>5% on SET)
4. Flamegraph confirms BTreeMap is no longer in top 10 CPU consumers
5. Isomorphism proof artifact is written and checked in
6. README updated with new numbers if improvement is significant","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:25:11.895427599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:20:34.479134166Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T02:20:34.478867962Z","close_reason":"Benchmark verified: SET p1=66K (70% Redis), SET p16=257K (30% Redis), all 43 conformance tests pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","perf","store"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-hs04.3","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-hs04","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:25:11.895427599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-hs04.3","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-hs04.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:27:27.573416302Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"frankenredis-hs8w","title":"Investigate br import-only rebuild hang on frankenredis JSONL","description":"Attempted to repair stale .beads/beads.db by rebuilding isolated temp DB from .beads/issues.jsonl: br sync --import-only --rebuild --json --db .beads/beads.db.rebuild_20260425T014843Z_codex_repair --no-auto-import --no-auto-flush. Command ran silently for over 60s and required SIGINT. Existing DB is malformed/stale: doctor reports page 2 never used, blocked_issues_cache stale, DB 780 vs JSONL 784+, and br show cannot find recent JSONL-only issues. sqlite3 CLI is unavailable; Python read-only sqlite query also fails with database disk image is malformed.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"codex-beads-repair","created_at":"2026-04-25T01:50:52.405269625Z","created_by":"codex-beads-repair","updated_at":"2026-04-25T01:50:52.405269625Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenredis-hs8w","title":"Investigate br import-only rebuild hang on frankenredis JSONL","description":"Attempted to repair stale .beads/beads.db by rebuilding isolated temp DB from .beads/issues.jsonl: br sync --import-only --rebuild --json --db .beads/beads.db.rebuild_20260425T014843Z_codex_repair --no-auto-import --no-auto-flush. Command ran silently for over 60s and required SIGINT. Existing DB is malformed/stale: doctor reports page 2 never used, blocked_issues_cache stale, DB 780 vs JSONL 784+, and br show cannot find recent JSONL-only issues. sqlite3 CLI is unavailable; Python read-only sqlite query also fails with database disk image is malformed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"codex-beads-repair","created_at":"2026-04-25T01:50:52.405269625Z","created_by":"codex-beads-repair","updated_at":"2026-04-28T03:45:49.747176994Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T03:45:49.745449472Z","close_reason":"Recovered: rebuilt .beads/beads.db from .beads/issues.jsonl. Procedure that worked (post-orphan-recovery): (1) cp .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.backup; (2) br doctor --repair (rebuilds blocked_issues_cache; doesn't touch corruption); (3) when 'br sync --import-only' fails with 'UNIQUE constraint failed: issues.id' due to duplicate-row corruption, mv .beads/beads.db{,-wal} aside; (4) br init --prefix frankenredis; (5) br sync --import-only --rebuild — imported 805/805 records cleanly. Post-rebuild 'br doctor': sqlite.integrity_check OK, counts.db_vs_jsonl OK (805/805), sync.metadata in sync. The original codex-beads-repair attempt hung because it was rebuilding into a separate temp DB while the main DB was concurrently locked — operating on the canonical .beads/beads.db path after moving the corrupt file aside avoids the lock contention.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-htt7","title":"Expand live Redis parity coverage for stable CONFIG admin surface","description":"Add a broader stable core_config live differential subset for deterministic CONFIG GET/pattern/wrong-arity/case-insensitive/hz-admin behavior against vendored Redis without overlapping FUNCTION or ACL follow-up work.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T21:08:19.223225232Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T21:12:45.307012473Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T21:12:45.305182069Z","close_reason":"Expanded stable live Redis CONFIG admin-surface coverage in standalone mode","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-hvfa","title":"Track real aof_enabled INFO persistence state","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T15:51:12.331027440Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T15:53:43.214620867Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T15:53:43.214342210Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-hx4n","title":"Add structured fr-protocol fuzz target for parser limits and RESP frame sequences","description":"Evidence: fuzz_resp_parser.rs currently throws raw bytes at parse_frame/parse_frame_with_config, and fuzz_resp_roundtrip.rs only checks encode/decode identity. That leaves a gap around structure-aware parser-limit boundaries and multi-frame consumed-length invariants in crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs:128 (parse_frame), 132 (parse_frame_with_config), 140 (parse_frame_internal), 180 (parse_bulk), and 211 (parse_array).\n\nNeed: add a structure-aware libFuzzer target that generates valid and near-valid RESP scalars/arrays plus trailing frame sequences, varies ParserConfig limits, and asserts invariants: consumed bytes stop exactly at the first frame, accepted frames stay accepted under looser limits, rejected frames at tight limits never panic, and back-to-back encoded frames can be iteratively parsed without drift.\n\nReal impl path: fuzz/Cargo.toml plus new fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_resp_configured_sequences.rs hitting fr-protocol directly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:17:19.110653598Z","created_by":"cod-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:34:26Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T20:34:26Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fr-protocol","fuzz","testing"]}
